title: Initialize a Tote Using the Aveva API Page
summary: Use the Initialize Tote Aveva API page to enter BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID, submit the request, and review the response returned by the system.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tote needs to be initialized for processing through the Initialize Tote Aveva API page shown in the source.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to assume a tote was initialized if the Response field shows an error or tote status instead of confirmation.
Do not use this procedure to resolve response conditions that the source does not explain how to correct.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the Initialize Tote Aveva API page, enter BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID, submit the request, and review the response.

safety_notes: The source presents this as an Aveva API page workflow and does not provide broader permission or access controls.
Do not assume initialization succeeded unless the Response field confirms it.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Initialize Tote Aveva API page
RMS reference for station ID as mentioned in the transcript

access_required: Access to the Initialize Tote Aveva API page
system_preconditions: BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID values are available before submitting the request.
external_references: RMS reference for station ID as mentioned in the transcript

visual_references.description: Initialize Tote Aveva API page showing BarcodeID, StationID, ToteID, Request to Initialize Tote, and Response areas.

steps.title: Open the Initialize Tote Aveva API page
steps.purpose: Access the interface used to submit a tote initialization request.
steps.instruction: Open the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.
steps.expected_result: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The page shows the BarcodeID, StationID, ToteID, Request to Initialize Tote, and Response areas.
steps.failure_condition: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page is not available or the expected fields and controls are not visible.

actions.label: Open initialize tote page
actions.exact_action: Open the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Initialize Tote Aveva API page with BarcodeID, StationID, ToteID, Request to Initialize Tote, and Response areas.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Initialize Tote Aveva API page cannot be accessed.
Stop or escalate if the expected fields or request/response areas are not present.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide shows an "Initialize Tote Aveva API page" used to initialize a tote for processing within the system.

steps.title: Enter the BarcodeID value
steps.purpose: Provide the barcode or tote number required by the initialization request.
steps.instruction: Enter the barcode or tote number in the BarcodeID field. Use the source note that the barcode and tote ID may be the same number.
steps.expected_result: The BarcodeID field contains the intended barcode or tote number.
steps.healthy_condition: The BarcodeID field is populated with the value to be used for the request.
steps.failure_condition: The BarcodeID field is blank or contains an incorrect value.

actions.label: Enter BarcodeID
actions.exact_action: Enter the barcode or tote number in the BarcodeID field.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: BarcodeID field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the correct barcode or tote number is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: User adds a barcode or tote number, then the station, then the tote ID; OCR lists BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID.

steps.title: Enter the StationID value
steps.purpose: Provide the station identifier required for the tote initialization request.
steps.instruction: Enter the StationID for the station on the rack, using the station ID as shown in RMS according to the transcript.
steps.expected_result: The StationID field contains the intended station identifier.
steps.healthy_condition: The StationID field is populated with the station ID to be used for the request.
steps.failure_condition: The StationID field is blank or contains an incorrect station ID.

actions.label: Enter StationID
actions.exact_action: Enter the StationID for the station on the rack.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: StationID field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the station ID cannot be determined from RMS as referenced by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: User adds a barcode or tote number, then the station, then the tote ID; OCR lists BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID.

steps.title: Enter the ToteID value
steps.purpose: Provide the tote identifier required by the initialization request.
steps.expected_result: The ToteID field contains the intended tote identifier.
steps.healthy_condition: The ToteID field is populated with the tote ID to be used for the request.
steps.failure_condition: The ToteID field is blank or contains an incorrect value.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: ToteID field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the correct tote ID is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: User adds a barcode or tote number, then the station, then the tote ID; OCR lists BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID.

steps.title: Submit the initialization request
steps.purpose: Send the entered tote information to the system for initialization.
steps.instruction: Click the Request to Initialize Tote button.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the request and returns a result in the Response field.
steps.healthy_condition: The request is submitted and the Response field updates with a returned result.
steps.failure_condition: The request is not submitted or no response is returned.

actions.label: Request tote initialization
actions.exact_action: Click the Request to Initialize Tote button.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Request to Initialize Tote button on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the request cannot be submitted.

steps.title: Review the Response field
steps.purpose: Confirm whether the tote initialization succeeded or identify a returned error or status.
steps.instruction: Review the Response field for the returned result, such as a confirmation message if successful or an error/status message if initialization fails.
steps.expected_result: The Response field indicates whether the tote was initialized successfully or provides an error/status explaining why it was not.
steps.healthy_condition: The Response field shows confirmation that the tote was initialized successfully.
steps.failure_condition: The Response field shows an error message or tote status instead of confirmation.

actions.label: Review response
actions.exact_action: Review the Response field for the returned result.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Response field on the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Response field shows an error or tote status instead of confirmation.
Stop or escalate if the tote cannot be initialized and the response indicates a status the user cannot resolve from the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Response field displays the result of initialization; successful requests show confirmation and failed requests show an error message or tote status.

success_criteria: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page accepts BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID input values.
The request is submitted using Request to Initialize Tote.
The Response field returns confirmation that the tote was initialized successfully.
healthy_conditions: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page is visible with BarcodeID, StationID, ToteID, Request to Initialize Tote, and Response areas.
BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID are entered as intended.
The Response field shows confirmation after the request is submitted.
failure_conditions: The Initialize Tote Aveva API page or expected fields are not available.
Required input values are missing or cannot be determined.
The Response field shows an error message or tote status instead of confirmation.
The tote cannot be initialized and the returned status is not resolvable from the source.
escalation_guidance: If the Response field shows an error or tote status instead of confirmation, do not assume initialization succeeded.
If the tote cannot be initialized and the response indicates a status the user cannot resolve from the source, escalate for further support.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Initialize Tote Aveva API page showing BarcodeID, StationID, ToteID, Request to Initialize Tote, and Response areas.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: User adds a barcode or tote number, then the station, then the tote ID; OCR lists BarcodeID, StationID, and ToteID.
Response field displays the result of initialization; successful requests show confirmation and failed requests show an error message or tote status.

missing_details: The source does not provide explicit navigation steps for reaching the Initialize Tote Aveva API page.
The source does not define required permissions or access approval for this page.
The source does not provide exact success text returned in the Response field.
The source does not provide exact error codes, error text, or remediation steps for specific failure responses.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
